metadata.source_title: Selling a company without a cyber certificate? Increasingly a deal breaker

text: ABN AMRO Editorial. Mergers and acquisitions are given an extra test in due diligence: cybersecurity. Buyers and investors are increasingly demanding demonstrable digital resilience – and this has everything to do with risks that would otherwise only become visible after an acquisition. Why cybersecurity has become decisive. Buyers do not want a digital time bomb: systems must be safe, incidents must be handled properly, chain risks must be controlled.

text: NIS2 as new standard. The European NIS2 directive sets requirements for thousands of companies and their chain. During acquisitions, buyers are increasingly looking to see whether the target company meets these requirements – or whether there will be a compliance issue. NIS2 Quality Mark as supporting document. For those looking to prepare for sales or financing, the [NIS2 Quality Mark (QM)](https://nis2qualitymark.eu/) offers a scalable, practical alternative to more stringent standards.

metadata.source_title: Selling a company without a cyber certificate? Increasingly a deal breaker

text: The European NIS2 directive sets requirements for thousands of companies and their chain. During acquisitions, buyers are increasingly looking to see whether the target company meets these requirements – or whether there will be a compliance issue. NIS2 Quality Mark as supporting document. For those looking to prepare for sales or financing, the [NIS2 Quality Mark (QM)](https://nis2qualitymark.eu/) offers a scalable, practical alternative to more stringent standards. This way you as an organization demonstrate that you manage risks - also in the chain.
